quick_facts.label: Conducting body
quick_facts.value: National Board of Examinations in Medical Sciences (NBEMS), an autonomous body under India’s Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, conducts NEET-PG.

quick_facts.label: Purpose
quick_facts.value: NEET-PG is the national eligibility-cum-ranking/entrance examination for admission to postgraduate medical pathways including MD/MS/PG Diploma, Post-MBBS DNB, Direct 6-year DrNB, and NBEMS Diploma courses.

quick_facts.label: 2026 examination date
quick_facts.value: For the 2026 session, NBEMS scheduled NEET-PG as a computer-based examination in a single shift on August 30, 2026; NBEMS stated that results would be declared by September 30, 2026.

quick_facts.label: Core Indian-graduate eligibility for 2026
quick_facts.value: Applicants need an MBBS degree or provisional MBBS pass certificate recognized under the NMC Act/PG Medical Education Regulations, permanent or provisional MBBS registration from NMC/MCI/State Medical Council, and completion of the one-year internship by September 30, 2026.

quick_facts.label: 2026 test format
quick_facts.value: The 2026 bulletin specifies 180 English-language MCQs, each with four options, in 3 hours 30 minutes. Marks are +4 correct, −1 incorrect, and 0 unattempted; the test has five time-restricted sections of 36 questions and 42 minutes each.

quick_facts.label: 2026 application fee
quick_facts.value: For 2026, the published examination fee is ₹3,500 for General/OBC/EWS candidates and ₹2,500 for SC/ST/PwBD candidates, excluding applicable payment-gateway charges. Fees and policies are session-specific and should be rechecked in the live bulletin.

sections.heading: What NEET-PG is for
paragraphs: NEET-PG is the single national eligibility-cum-ranking examination prescribed for admission to postgraduate medical education. For the 2026–27 admission session, its stated scope includes MD/MS/PG Diploma programmes, Post-MBBS DNB programmes, Direct 6-year DrNB programmes, and NBEMS Diploma programmes.

The NEET-PG score is used in admission processes, but NBEMS’s role ends with conducting the test, declaring results, and transmitting results to the designated counselling authority. Taking or qualifying the examination does not itself create a right to a seat; the relevant counselling/admitting authority verifies documents and applies its own applicable eligibility, reservation, and institutional rules.
bullets: For All India Quota, deemed/central university and all-India open DNB seats, counselling information is handled through the Medical Counselling Committee (MCC) of DGHS.
State quota and in-service-seat counselling are handled by the relevant state authority. Candidates should independently check domicile, reservation, and institution-specific conditions.

sections.heading: Who can apply
paragraphs: For an Indian medical graduate in NEET-PG 2026, the central requirements are a recognized MBBS degree or provisional pass certificate, permanent or provisional registration for the MBBS qualification issued by NMC/the former MCI/a State Medical Council, and completion of the compulsory one-year internship on or before September 30, 2026. Candidates whose internship finishes after that date, or whose qualification is not recognized as prescribed, are not eligible for the 2026 process.

Indian citizens and Overseas Citizens of India whose primary medical qualification was obtained outside India must, in addition, have qualified FMGE under the Screening Test Regulations, be registered with NMC/MCI/State Medical Council, and satisfy the applicable internship deadline. Foreign-national rules are more specific, including NMC recognition of the basic qualification and, where applicable, Ministry of Home Affairs security clearance and documentation for temporary registration.
bullets: Eligibility shown at the admit-card stage is provisional; verification occurs in counselling/admission.

For 2026, the published qualifying thresholds are the 50th percentile for General/EWS, 45th percentile for UR-PwBD, and 40th percentile for SC/ST/OBC including PwBD in those categories. These are qualifying standards, not a guarantee of admission.

sections.heading: Exam structure and scoring
paragraphs: NEET-PG 2026 is scheduled as a computer-based test on August 30, 2026 in one shift. The paper contains 180 multiple-choice questions in English, with four response options per question; candidates select the correct/best/most appropriate response. Total testing time is 3 hours 30 minutes.

The 2026 scoring rule is +4 for a correct response, −1 for an incorrect response, and 0 for an unanswered question. NBEMS describes five time-restricted sections—Groups A through E—with 36 questions and 42 minutes per section; once a section’s allotted time ends, a candidate cannot revisit or modify its responses.
bullets: The syllabus is based on the MBBS curriculum issued by NMC/the former Medical Council of India.
NBEMS has indicated that a computer-based-test demo will be made available on its website, tentatively from August 15, 2026.

sections.heading: Applying for the 2026 session
paragraphs: NBEMS published the 2026 application window as July 1, 2026 at 5:00 PM through July 21, 2026 at 11:55 PM. The only valid submission route is the online application through the NBEMS process; applications submitted by another mode or after the window closes are not accepted. The 2026 notice also says that early submission does not confer priority for a particular test state, city, or centre.

Applicants should enter their information carefully, upload the required images/documents, choose the required test-state preferences, pay online, and confirm that payment status is successful. NBEMS says an application is completed only after successful payment and advises candidates to retain the submitted application PDF. The 2026 admit card is scheduled to go live on August 27, 2026.
bullets: Published 2026 fee context: ₹3,500 for General/OBC/EWS and ₹2,500 for SC/ST/PwBD, plus any applicable payment-gateway charges.
For 2026, candidates must select three preferred test states; the first must be the state of the correspondence address supplied in the form.
The fee is generally non-refundable and cannot be carried forward after a submitted application; applicants should consult the current bulletin before paying.

sections.heading: Preparation approach grounded in the official scheme
paragraphs: Build revision around the NMC/erstwhile MCI MBBS curriculum, because that is the syllabus base stated in the 2026 bulletin. Use timed practice that reflects the current 2026 format: 36 questions in 42 minutes across five locked sections. This is especially important because a completed section cannot be revisited or edited.

Plan response decisions around the scoring rule rather than treating every unanswered item as equivalent to a wrong answer: correct answers earn four marks, wrong answers lose one mark, and unattempted items receive zero. Before test day, use the NBEMS computer-based-test demo when it becomes available to practise navigation and time-restricted sections.
bullets: Prioritize retrieval practice and error review across the MBBS knowledge areas rather than relying only on passive reading.
During timed mocks, rehearse a section-level workflow: answer high-confidence items, mark review candidates while time remains, and complete all revision before that section locks.

sections.heading: What follows the result
paragraphs: NBEMS scheduled declaration of the NEET-PG 2026 result by September 30, 2026. Candidates can download scorecards using their login credentials; the bulletin states that scorecards will be available for six months from result declaration. NBEMS also states that it will publish the answer key and each candidate’s recorded responses in accordance with the cited Supreme Court order.

A qualifying result enables participation in the applicable counselling pathway only if all additional rules are met. For All India Quota, deemed/central university, and all-India open DNB seats, MCC/DGHS publishes counselling procedures; state quota eligibility, ranks, reservation benefits, and admission rules remain subject to the relevant state/UT or institution.
bullets: NEET-PG 2026 qualifying cut-offs published in the bulletin are percentile-based; competitive rank, seat availability, category rules, preferences, and counselling choices determine actual allotment.
A candidate who merely appears in or passes NEET-PG is not automatically eligible for every state quota, private university, or institute.
